title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience through the AUKUS partnership. Full operational capability for DARC is targeted for 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, aiming to match their performance even in urban areas. The document positions Starlink Mobile as a future wireless challenger beyond its current use as a backup for remote regions.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also announced plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab wins first GEO satellite production contract from U.S. Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $90 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to build and operate two GEO satellites with optical payloads. This marks Rocket Lab's first GEO satellite production contract.

title: Ground equipment problem scrubs Starship launch attempt
summary: SpaceX canceled the first launch attempt of its newest Starship vehicle on May 21 due to a ground equipment issue. The launch was scrubbed before liftoff.

title: Sixth Varda mission successfully returns
summary: Varda Space Industries successfully completed its sixth reentry mission on May 18, advancing pharmaceutical research and hypersonic testing. The company is balancing commercial and research objectives in its space missions.

title: Space Force on path to double active-duty force by 2030
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to double its active-duty force by 2030. The growth rate is limited by training capacity and the establishment of new operational units.

title: Starship underpins SpaceX’s growth ambitions
summary: SpaceX's upcoming Starship test flight is crucial for the company's growth ambitions, as highlighted in its prospectus. The vehicle's success is seen as a key driver for SpaceX's future plans.

title: The internet was ‘too expensive’ too
summary: The article compares the early cost challenges of internet infrastructure to those faced by new space infrastructure platforms. It highlights that early systems often have high costs due to bespoke designs, immature supply chains, and lack of scaling.

title: CEO Series: Open Cosmos’ Rafel Jorda Siquier on finding a niche in space
summary: Open Cosmos' CEO Rafel Jorda Siquier discusses combining Earth observation, connectivity, and IoT into a single platform in an interview with Space Minds. The conversation highlights the company's strategy to find a niche in the space industry.

title: NASA is updating its Artemis moon base plan. You can find out how on May 26.
summary: NASA is updating its Artemis moon base plan and will announce details on May 26. The update will be livestreamed in the afternoon.

title: Scientists thought Jupiter's moon Europa was ejecting water. Now they're not so sure
summary: Scientists have revised their initial belief that Jupiter's moon Europa was ejecting water vapor plumes, now finding the evidence less conclusive than previously thought.

title: The moon will 'swallow' a star on May 23. But will you be able to see it?
summary: On May 23, the Moon will occult the star Regulus, blocking its light for some observers. This rare celestial event may be visible depending on location.

title: This cryptocurrency billionaire will fly SpaceX's 1st private Starship to Mars, but when?
summary: SpaceX plans to launch a private flyby mission to Mars led by cryptocurrency billionaire Chun Wang, though the exact launch date remains uncertain.

title: SpaceX's 1st Starship V3 megarocket launch scrubbed at last minute. Sorry, Nicki Minaj
summary: SpaceX's first Starship V3 megarocket launch was scrubbed at the last minute due to technical issues. The planned maiden flight on May 21 was canceled before liftoff.

title: Rocket Lab launches private Japanese Earth-observing satellite early on Friday (May 22)
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite for Synspective early on May 22. The launch was broadcast live.

title: We talk to 'For All Mankind' star Mireille Enos about crafting Happy Valley's tough Martian cop
summary: The news discusses an interview with actress Mireille Enos, who stars in 'For All Mankind' and 'Happy Valley', highlighting her portrayal of a tough Martian cop character who avoids attention and promotions.

title: Epic 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' merch has landed. 'This is the way' to Grogu Crocs, Lego & more
summary: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu merchandise has been released ahead of the movie's theater launch. The collection includes items like Grogu Crocs and Lego sets.

title: This exoplanet weather forecast by the James Webb Space Telescope calls for sandy skies and a clear (alien) sunset
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope observed weather changes on a distant hot Jupiter exoplanet, transitioning from cloudy mornings to clear skies by dusk. This marks a significant milestone in exoplanet atmospheric studies.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with the National Space Policy. The changes aim to improve focus and efficiency in achieving national space objectives.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: A new material discovered at NASA's Glenn Research Center could enable astronauts to melt Moon rocks and extract valuable resources like metals and oxygen. This innovation aims to support future lunar missions by reducing payload weight through in-situ resource utilization.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes. The image also features foreground stars with diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket carrying the Katalyst robotic spacecraft. The LINK servicing spacecraft is scheduled to launch in June to rendezvous with the observatory.

title: NASA Joins Artemis Accords Workshop as Global Signings Rise
summary: NASA participated in an Artemis Accords workshop in Lima, Peru, as six new countries joined the international coalition. The event highlights the growing global commitment to safe and responsible exploration of the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

title: NASA Seeks Interest for Artemis Mission CubeSats
summary: NASA is seeking interest from organizations to launch CubeSats on future Artemis missions by responding to an RFI by June 1. The initiative aims to support science and technology investigations that advance human space exploration.

title: NASA’s AWE Completes Mission to Study Earth’s Effect on Space Weather
summary: NASA's AWE mission successfully completed its two-year study of Earth's atmospheric gravity waves and their impact on space weather. The instrument, installed on the ISS, exceeded its planned mission duration.

title: NASA Wins Four Telly Awards for Artemis Moon Coverage, More
summary: NASA won four Telly Awards for its Artemis II mission coverage, highlighting the historic return of humans to the Moon. The awards recognize the agency's video productions documenting the mission.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. Dust filaments and reddish-brown clumps obscure parts of the galaxy, while distant background galaxies are visible through its outer regions.

title: Starship Flight 12 Scrubs Due to Ship Quick Disconnect Issues
summary: Starship Flight 12 was scrubbed due to issues with the ship quick disconnect. This marks a delay in SpaceX's ongoing development efforts.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing development and improvements. It is presented as a key advancement in the booster's design for future missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to low Earth orbit. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The launch follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are preparing for nine launches in the week of May 11, marking a busy period for space missions. The launches span various activities in the United States and China.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and education.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for a Mars mission, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on schedule for a launch in early 2027 to search for terrestrial planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that existing military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double funding for space initiatives, stating that current public funds are insufficient despite recent increases. The call highlights concerns within the industry about inadequate financial support for growth.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey revealed that improved Moon-related technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey was conducted two years ago to identify key technical challenges in the space industry.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two new Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. The missions are designed to demonstrate that significant scientific achievements can be accomplished on a smaller budget.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67B in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO. The company is expected to go public next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system will accelerate the nuclear arms race rather than protect America. The system's intended purpose is to provide defense, but critics believe it will provoke further escalation.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve collaborations with Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The report highlights concerns over these scientific partnerships.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in engineering production, aiming to reduce inefficiencies and stalls. The company is shifting from traditional design reviews to a more intelligent decision-making framework.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, necessitating up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, and high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development and deployment efforts with this capital.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify innovative solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of geospatial information.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport operations. This collaboration aims to enhance airport management tools using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first public glimpse into its financials. This marks a significant step toward going public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: The upcoming World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) in 2027 is expected to focus heavily on satellite-related agenda items. U.S. space executives express concerns about China's influence due to its hosting of the event.

title: Redwire Taps Voyager for Subcontract for DARPA Otter Mission
summary: Redwire has awarded Voyager Technologies a subcontract for the DARPA Otter mission to supply its high-precision Acceleration Measurement System (AMS). The mission aims to advance space technology for defense applications.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs Want Multiple Constellations for D2D Service
summary: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs discussed their satellite strategies and emphasized the need for multiple constellations to support direct-to-device (D2D) services after forming a joint venture for this purpose.

title: Iceye Launches New Solution for Banking Sector
summary: Iceye has launched a new natural catastrophe monitoring solution targeting the international banking sector. The offering aims to provide critical data for risk assessment and financial decision-making.

title: HackerOne Exec Explains Key Security Trends in the Age of AI
summary: Generative AI is being weaponized by hackers, posing significant cybersecurity challenges. HackerOne, a key player in cybersecurity, is addressing these emerging threats.
